entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench, a crop studied for its seedling emergence under high temperature conditions.

entities.name: HIGH SOIL TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Soil temperatures exceeding 45°C that inhibit the field emergence of sorghum.

entities.name: SEEDLING EMERGENCE
entities.description: The process of seedlings emerging from the soil, which is inhibited by high soil temperatures.

entities.name: SEMIARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographical region where sorghum field emergence is inhibited by high soil temperatures.

entities.name: EMBRYO PROTEIN SYNTHESIS
entities.description: A rapid technique used to assess sorghum emergence by measuring protein synthesis in embryos using 14C-labeled amino acids.

entities.name: LANDRACE ACCESSIONS
entities.description: Traditional sorghum varieties used to evaluate the relationship between embryo protein synthesis and emergence.

entities.name: COMMERCIALLY AVAILABLE LINES
entities.description: Commercial sorghum varieties used to evaluate the relationship between embryo protein synthesis and emergence.
